regions.eastern-caribbean: Eastern Caribbean briefing for March 13, 2026: San Juan reports 73°F with light ESE winds at 4 knots and partly cloudy skies; a 30% chance of isolated showers exists. Expect increasing east winds up to 16 knots through the period with temperatures near 83°F. Rain showers are likely today with improved conditions tomorrow, though scattered showers may return by day three. Mariners should monitor localized rain squalls near San Juan, St. Thomas, and St. Croix; small craft operators should remain cautious in exposed anchorages due to easterly breeze and periodic showers. Cruise passengers should keep light rain gear handy, while fishing captains should plan for variable visibility and brief wet spells offshore.
regions.western-caribbean: Western Caribbean briefing for March 13, 2026: Current conditions in Cozumel show 76°F with light NNW winds at 4 knots and isolated rain showers, minimal rain chance at 5 percent. Over the next three days, expect increasing thunderstorm activity with a high rain probability of 88 percent on Day 1 and 78 percent on Day 2, accompanied by light to moderate winds shifting from NNE to ESE at 6 knots. By Day 3, thunderstorms will taper to scattered rain showers with a 40 percent chance and winds veering SSE at 11 knots. Mariners should anticipate localized heavy rainfall and reduced visibility near Cozumel, Costa Maya, and Belize City; secure loose gear and monitor radar for sudden thunderstorm development. Yacht and fishing captains are advised to avoid open waters during peak thunderstorm periods, especially approaching Cozumel and Roatan, and consider sheltered anchorages in Grand Cayman or Falmouth until conditions improve.
regions.bahamas: Bahamas region weather for March 13, 2026: Currently in Nassau, clear skies with light SSW winds at 4 knots and 70°F; rain chance negligible. Expect increasing cloud cover today with southerly winds 7 knots and temperatures rising to 81°F; rain chances remain low near 8 percent. By days two and three, light drizzle is likely with southeast to east-southeast winds increasing to 11-17 knots and rain chances up to 29 percent. Captains should monitor gradual wind shifts and moderate drizzle conditions for safe navigation around Freeport, Bimini, Berry Islands, and Turks & Caicos; fishing operations may find calmer seas today but prepare for light rain and reduced visibility midweek. Passengers should plan for mostly dry conditions today with overcast skies and light rain possible later in the period.
regions.southern-caribbean:
Southern Caribbean Weather Briefing for March 13, 2026:

Aruba, Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena will experience moderate easterly trade winds between 13 and 18 knots, with partly cloudy to overcast skies. Rain chances remain low to moderate, generally under 30 percent, with isolated light showers possible but no severe weather expected. Mariners should note smooth seas with no Small Craft Advisory conditions currently forecast. Yacht and fishing captains operating near Aruba and Bonaire can expect stable conditions for morning departures; however, monitor localized cloud cover for brief rain squalls. Cruise passengers should plan for mild, breezy weather with comfortable temperatures near 78 to 81 degrees Fahrenheit and carry light rain gear when ashore.
regions.central-caribbean: Central Caribbean weather will remain stable today with light ENE winds around 5 to 7 knots and temperatures near 78 to 80 degrees Fahrenheit. Expect partly cloudy skies and minimal rain chances under 10 percent through Day 2, shifting to overcast conditions with increasing ESE winds up to 13 knots and a 30 percent chance of showers by Day 3. Roatan, Belize City, Grand Cayman, Cozumel, and Costa Maya will experience generally calm seas and favorable conditions for cruising, fishing, and yacht operations. Captains should monitor the slight increase in showers toward Day 3, especially for anchorage selection in exposed areas like Belize’s reefs; maintain routine weather checks and ensure rain gear is available for passengers during excursions.
regions.lesser-antilles: Lesser Antilles update for March 13, 2026: Barbados reports steady ENE winds at 16 knots with overcast skies and minimal rain chances today, trending to breezier conditions with 20+ knot winds by Day 3. Expect generally stable weather across St. Lucia, Martinique, Dominica, Antigua, St. Kitts, St. Maarten, St. Vincent, and Grenada, with light to moderate trade winds and scattered showers increasing toward midweek. Mariners should secure loose gear and monitor wind shifts as easterly winds strengthen, especially near exposed anchorages in St. Maarten and Antigua. Fishing captains should anticipate calmer seas today but prepare for increasing swell and variable cloud cover over the next 48 hours. Passengers advised to plan outdoor activities early and stay weather aware for isolated showers later in the week.
regions.los-angeles: Los Angeles and adjacent ports are currently clear with light west winds at 4 knots and temps near 66°F. Expect a warm day tomorrow with west winds increasing to 11 knots and overcast skies, but no rain or marine wind warnings. Conditions cool on Sunday with SSE winds around 8 knots and remain overcast; Monday sees a return to warmer temperatures near 82°F with SSW winds near 9 knots. Mariners should prepare for moderate westerly to southerly wind shifts; small craft operators and fishing vessels should monitor seas for any increases but no advisories are in effect. Catalina Island anchorages remain calm, though some overcast may reduce daytime visibility—exercise standard caution when navigating confined harbor areas.
regions.ensenada: Ensenada region conditions this morning are calm with clear skies, light east-southeast winds at 2 knots, and temperatures near 69°F. Expect a mild warm-up today with southwest winds increasing to 6 knots under overcast skies and no rain, favoring stable navigation at Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos. Fog is likely on day two with west-southwest winds at 6 knots and cooler temps near 63°F; mariners should maintain vigilance for reduced visibility in coastal areas and harbors. Fishing and yacht captains should plan for light to moderate winds and low seas through the period, securing vessels overnight due to persistent overcast conditions.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Cabo San Lucas and surrounding ports will experience mild conditions today with clear skies, light NNW winds at 8 knots, and no rain—ideal for all marine operations. Over the next three days, winds will shift slightly NW and increase to 17 knots by Day 3, with overcast to partly cloudy skies but no precipitation expected. Mariners should note stable sea conditions with no advisories in effect; anchorage at San Jose del Cabo and La Paz remains safe and calm. Fishing captains and yacht operators can plan for smooth navigation and excellent visibility, while cruise passengers should enjoy comfortable temperatures in the mid-70s with light to moderate sea breezes.
regions.mazatlan: Mazatlan and surrounding ports including Topolobampo and Altata will experience mild marine conditions today with light NNW winds at 3 knots, partly cloudy skies, and no precipitation expected. Winds will shift to WNW and then NW over the next three days, increasing gradually to 11 knots by day three with clear skies and zero rain chance. Mariners can expect calm seas ideal for fishing and safe maneuvering in all harbor areas; small craft operators should maintain routine vigilance but no advisories are in effect. Cruise passengers can enjoy comfortable temperatures near 75-77°F with low humidity and minimal weather impacts for shore excursions.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Puerto Vallarta region conditions remain stable for March 13, 2026. Winds are light from the northwest at 2 knots, increasing to 6-8 knots west-northwest over the next three days with partly to mostly clear skies and zero rain chance. Mariners operating near Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala can expect calm seas, ideal for cruising and fishing activities. Captains should maintain routine anchor checks in Yelapa and Chacala, where sheltered coves provide good holding, and anticipate smooth navigation with minimal weather impacts through March 15.
regions.western-mediterranean: Western Mediterranean briefing for March 13, 2026: Barcelona reports clear skies, light northwest winds at 2 knots, and 50°F currently, with no rain expected today. Expect fog in Valencia and Palma de Mallorca tomorrow morning with southwest winds near 7 knots, reducing visibility—exercise caution when maneuvering or approaching port. Rain showers with strong easterly winds around 9 knots are forecast for day two, notably affecting Ibiza and Malaga; secure light craft and prepare for slick decks. By day three, overcast conditions with moderate westerly winds around 7 knots will prevail in Cadiz, Lisbon, and Gibraltar, with minimal rain; good conditions for fishing and cruising. Monitor local fog advisories closely in low-lying harbor areas, especially near Barcelona and Palma de Mallorca.
regions.central-mediterranean: Central Mediterranean briefing for March 13, 2026: Naples reports mild conditions at 56°F with light northeast winds around 3 knots and partly cloudy skies; rain chances are minimal today. Expect increasing cloud cover and a rise in south winds up to 7 knots tomorrow, with a moderate 28 percent chance of rain—overcast conditions will persist into day three with east winds near 7 knots and a low rain risk. Mariners navigating near Naples and nearby ports such as Civitavecchia, Livorno, and Genoa should prepare for limited visibility due to fog on day two, especially in sheltered anchorages. Cruise and yacht captains should monitor updated forecasts for potential light rain and reduced visibility, adjusting schedules accordingly; fishing operations will benefit from calm winds today but should remain alert for overcast and fog conditions midweek.
regions.eastern-mediterranean:
Eastern Mediterranean briefing for March 13, 2026:

Athens/Piraeus reports clear skies, light NNW winds at 1 knot, and 55°F. Expect fog developing over the next three days with light NNE to ESE winds 5-6 knots and no precipitation. Visibility restrictions are likely in early mornings around Athens, Santorini, Mykonos, and Rhodes, so mariners should exercise caution navigating narrow channels or approaching ports during fog. Yacht and fishing captains should monitor visibility closely near Corfu, Izmir, and Haifa where similar conditions may prevail; calm seas will allow for safe operations but reduced visibility could delay departures. No marine wind warnings are in effect at this time.
